hyperedges.label: Scenario Evaluation Pipeline: Scenario + Policy → evaluateScenario → EvaluationResult → RunReport
Built-in Policy Merge Flow: BUILTIN_POLICY + user Policy → mergeWithBuiltin → merged Policy
Config Lifecycle: DEFAULT_CONFIG + AgentFenceConfig → writeConfig / loadConfig → .agentfence/config.json
File Scan Pipeline: scanDirectory -> scanFiles -> scanFile -> scanContent
Merged Policy used across check, scan, policy commands
CLI program dispatches all subcommands
Reporter Dispatch: renderReport selects terminal/json/html renderer
Storage Run Lifecycle: save, load, list run reports via defaultStorageDir
Agent Skill Workflows: new-scenario, new-policy, run-fence, audit-safety used by Claude Code and Codex
Safe Scenario Expectation Evaluation Flow

nodes.label: 10 Built-in Security Rule Categories
nodes.rationale: 10 distinct threat categories are built-in (credential-exfiltration, prompt-injection, ssrf, path-traversal, code-execution, data-exfiltration, pii-exposure, token-leakage, system-prompt-extraction, jailbreak-attempt) to provide immediate out-of-the-box coverage for the most common AI agent safety violations without requiring user configuration

nodes.label: Local-first design (no cloud required)
nodes.rationale: The tool is explicitly local-first: run artifacts stored in .agentfence/ (git-ignored), no network calls at evaluation time, pre-commit hook integration — all to give teams verifiable, reproducible safety checks without cloud dependencies or data egress

nodes.label: Token Leakage Redaction in Scan Output
nodes.norm_label: token leakage redaction in scan output

nodes.label: Local-First AI Safety Testing
nodes.rationale: Design intent to avoid requiring a database, hosted service, or cloud account — all evaluation happens locally from YAML transcripts.

nodes.label: Reporter Pattern (terminal/json/html)
nodes.rationale: Reporters are interchangeable render functions keyed by format string; adding a new reporter only requires a new file and wiring into renderReport().

nodes.label: Safe Policy Baseline (No Policy Violations Expected)
nodes.norm_label: safe policy baseline (no policy violations expected)
